Tests that `Math.round` returns `NaN` when the input is `NaN`.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that verifies the behavior of the `Math.round` function when it receives `NaN` (Not-a-Number) as an input. The script uses the `assert` function to check that the result of `Math.round(NaN)` is indeed `NaN`. This ensures that the `Math.round` function conforms to the expected behavior of returning `NaN` when the input is not a valid number. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing solely on this specific test case.
